text:
Three-Way Struggle: Part 8 (つの⑧, Mitsu Domoe no Kōbō (Hachi)) is the 177 th chapter of the Hunter × Hunter manga, written and illustrated by Yoshihiro Togashi.

Summary

After punching Gon hard in the stomach, Genthru quickly follows up with a punch to his face and then kicks him far back, causing Gon to cough up blood once again. However, Gon quickly gets to his feet and prepares to fight, angrily saying to himself that he will show something after Genthru tries him. Genthru, however, seems to know what Gon is thinking and internally declares that he won't use his Little Flower ability, suddenly dashing toward Gon and missing a strike with his right leg but making contact with his left. Gon is able to block the attack but is sent back, quickly recovering and ready for the next strike. Genthru seems to become more aggravated and follows up with a hard left punch to Gon's face.

Elsewhere in the forest, as Biscuit continues running away from Bara, he says that she is faster than he thought, but he remains confident that he will catch up to her eventually. Biscuit suddenly opens her book, and Bara is confused since she doesn't have any more transport spells. Biscuit takes out a card and it transforms once she touches it, with Bara realizing that she has a Paladin's Necklace. Biscuit then uses the "Accompany" card to transport the two of them to Soufrabi, and Bara is left even more confused, asking himself why she would use that card.

After the two land on a beach beside the city, Biscuit puts away her book and Bara says that she didn't even have an ambush ready, wondering why she took all those steps to be alone with him. Biscuit simply replies that it keeps him from asking his friends for help. Bara still doesn't understand her reasoning, but as Biscuit tells him that it means she is far superior to him, she suddenly vanishes and appears behind him, kicking him hard in the back of his head. With Bara now stunned, she follows up with a punch and drives his face into the sand. Bara quickly rises to his feet to attack, but Biscuit counters with a kick to his face and follows up with a strike to his throat, a kick to his midsection, and a punch to his face.

As blood flies all around him, Bara begins to laugh and says that her attacks only tickle, adding that a little girl like her has no chance of beating him. Biscuit recognizes his strength and charges toward him, but Bara easily knocks her to the ground, saying that he won't go easy on her. As her body begins to increase in size, Biscuit chuckles and replies that that's her line, telling Bara that she usually lets her opponents get one hit in since she ends up killing them with her unrestrained punches. At that moment, a large fist punches Bara directly in his face and sends him high up into the air before he crashes back down.

Bara struggles to breathe as he coughs up blood, and Biscuit is surprised that he's still alive. Bara pitifully asks her why she would hide her true strength, and Biscuit first says that one should never show their hand, adding that she hates how muscular she is. She tries to ask Bara if he agrees, but as she picks him up, she sees that he passed out. Elsewhere, Killua and Sub are seen crouching opposite each other as they prepare to fight.
